role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; the LoC oral history is the primary archival source for this catalog page.
ai_reading: Jennifer Lawson's neighbor counts are moderate (peak 85), with Charles McLaurin (entry 17) as the top match and the broader cluster including Dr. Doris Derby (#32), Gwendolyn M. Patton (#52), H. Jack Geiger (partial read) (#54).
biographical_paragraph: Jennifer Lawson's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 59 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. The LoC transcript carries a not-auditable audit tier: the catalog cannot independently certify the transcript against primary sources for this entry, and any specific biographical or quoted claims should be confirmed against external archives before citation. Independent biographical sourcing for an extended orientation paragraph is pending; the strongest signal currently available is the embedding pattern named in the AI's reading above.
